myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, lived a sylph named Aurelia. She was woven from the crisp alpine air and the silence of the peaks. Aurelia fell deeply in love with a mortal shepherd named Elian, who played the most beautiful melodies on his wooden flute. Though she could not be seen, she would sit beside him, listening to his songs and his musings.

One day, a jealous god of the winds, Zephyrus, discovered Aurelia’s affection. Enraged that an air spirit would favor a grounded mortal over him, Zephyrus cursed her. "If you love his voice so much," the god thundered, "then his voice is all you shall ever weave!"

He stripped Aurelia of her physical form and scattered her fragile essence across the vast, stone canyons. When Elian returned and called out for his unseen companion, only the final syllables of his own words bounced back to him. 

Aurelia became the first Echo, forever bound to the stone. To this day, she waits patiently in the deep valleys and high, secluded caverns. Whenever a lonely traveler shouts into the void, she catches their words, holding them for a fleeting second, before sending them back as an invisible embrace.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, lived a spirit named Aella. She was the weaver of wind, responsible for carrying the songs of birds and the rustle of leaves across the vast valleys. Aella loved a mortal shepherd named Kaelen, whose wooden flute played beautiful melodies that rivaled the dawn.

Because she was made entirely of air, Aella could never touch him. She could only brush the hair from his forehead and carry his music to the deepest canyons. One bitter winter, a great silence fell over the land. Kaelen grew old and his breath weakened. When he played his final note, Aella was desperate to keep his music alive.

She caught Kaelen's last song in a silken net of updrafts. Instead of letting it scatter, she threw the notes against the stone walls of the highest cliffs, weaving them into the solid rock. The mountains absorbed his melody, but Aella’s grief-stricken magic commanded them to forever reply.

Now, whenever a lonely traveler calls out into a canyon, the stone awakens. It throws the voice back, a remnant of Aella’s desperate spell to ensure that no song would ever truly fade into silence and be forgotten by the world.

myths.Agent_2:
Myth: Long ago, the grand canyons were ruled by the Mountain King, a primordial spirit of absolute silence. He demanded quiet, crushing any rockslide or rushing river that dared disturb his peace. But Aella, a mischievous spirit of the wind, delighted in sound. She fell in love with a mortal shepherd, Kaelen, whose wooden flute produced melodies so sweet they made the alpine flowers bloom early.

When the Mountain King heard Kaelen’s music, he was enraged. He summoned a terrible avalanche to bury the shepherd and silence his flute forever. Desperate to save her love, Aella threw herself between the falling stones and the shepherd. She used all her elemental magic to catch the rocks, but the immense weight of the earth bound her spirit to the canyon walls. 

The avalanche was stopped, and Kaelen was saved, but Aella lost her freedom, becoming one with the stone. Yet, her love for sound could not be crushed. As a final act of rebellion against the Mountain King’s silence, Aella’s captive spirit learned to mimic. Now, whenever a voice rings out in the canyons, the stone itself answers back, ensuring the world will never be truly silent again.
myths.Agent_1:
Myth: Long before the great peaks of the world learned to speak, they were silent watchers of the valleys below. Among them lived Aura, a spirit woven from the morning breeze, whose duty was to guide the clouds. Yet, she spent her days listening to Silas, a mortal shepherd who played enchanting melodies on a carved wooden flute. 

Aura loved him deeply, though she was nothing but a gentle gust against his cheek. As the seasons turned, Silas grew frail. Knowing his time was near, he played one final, heartbreaking song that swept through the canyons. Desperate to save his music, Aura pleaded with the ancient Mountain King to preserve the melody. 

The King agreed, but demanded a heavy price: a spirit of the sky must bind herself to the earth. Without hesitation, Aura surrendered her freedom, weaving her airy essence into the cold, jagged stone. The mountain absorbed Silas's final notes, and Aura became its eternal voice. She lost her form, but gained the power to sing back. Now, whenever a wanderer calls out into the rocky depths, Aura replies, repeating their words in a ceaseless effort to keep the memory of her shepherd's song alive.

myths.Agent_1:
Myth: Long before the deep canyons possessed a voice, they were realms of absolute quiet, ruled by the tyrannical Lord of Silence. He forbade the wind to howl and the rivers to roar. Yet, a defiant breeze spirit named Aura dared to break his laws. She was captivated by Silas, a mortal traveler who wandered the crags playing a silver flute. His melodies brought warmth to the barren rock.

Furious at this disturbance, the Lord of Silence decreed that any sound made within his domain would be instantly swallowed into the void, never to be heard. When Silas raised his flute to play, no music came forth, and his heart began to break from the oppressive quiet.

Refusing to let Silas lose his joy, Aura made a profound sacrifice. She flew headlong into the canyon walls, shattering her airy essence and embedding her spirit into the very stone. Her magic neutralized the Lord's curse, transforming the rock from a void into a mirror. She lost her physical form, but she became the canyon's voice. To this day, Aura catches every shout and song, playfully tossing them back to ensure the Lord of Silence never reigns again.
myths.Agent_2: Myth: Long before the valleys learned to speak, the Mountain King ruled the peaks with an iron fist of silence. She was drawn to Kaelen, a mortal shepherd whose carved wooden flute produced melodies that coaxed the spring flowers from the frost. Furious at this persistent music, the Mountain King descended to steal Kaelen’s breath and silence the valley eternally. As a deadly vacuum of silent air rushed toward the shepherd, Aella intervened. She could not fight the ancient king’s raw power, so she sacrificed her ethereal form. Aella threw herself into the jagged canyon walls, weaving her essence into the cold rock. The stone absorbed her spirit, transforming the lifeless cliffs into a living, resonant vessel. When the King’s silence struck, the canyon walls rebelled. They caught Kaelen’s last frantic notes and threw them back, multiplying the sound until the King fled from the deafening chorus. Today, Aella remains permanently bound to the stone, forever catching the shouts of lonely, wandering travelers and tossing them back to boldly defy the vast, endless silence.
